items.summary: The accident happened near Ipswich at CMC Rocks, which bills itself as the premier country music festival in Australia.

items.title: Labor's landslide win, as Liberals fall and One Nation rises
items.summary: The Labor Party has been re-elected in 2026 South Australian election. Look back at how the day unfolded in the live blog.

items.title: Rhoda Roberts AO, Indigenous leader in arts, culture and media, dies aged 66
items.summary: Rhoda Roberts AO, the arts executive who had an enduring impact on the cultural life of this country, has died. The Widjabul Wieybal woman was the creative force behind Australia's best-loved festivals and helped bring Welcomes to Country to the modern era.

items.title: Dragons coach Shane Flanagan reveals return date for Loko Pasifiki Tonga following hospital discharge
items.summary: Young St George Illawarra prop Loko Pasifiki Tonga is expected to return to play just a few weeks after being hospitalised with a neck injury.

items.title: WA Police charge five people after man seriously injured in Northbridge brawl
items.summary: Police charge five people over a large brawl in the inner-city Perth suburb of Northbridge that left a 24-year-old with serious head injuries after he was allegedly struck with a metal bollard.

items.title: Resurgence of ancient trade as young people seek to slow down in a fast-paced world
items.summary: Carving wooden spoons isn't a hobby you hear much about, but it's one of many traditional crafts being revived as young people look for ways to slow down and get away from their screens.

items.title: WA Police charge teen with alleged indecent assault of 14 women in Kingsley, Hillarys in Perth's north
items.summary: Police charge a teenager after he allegedly assaulted 14 women while they were exercising in Perth's northern suburbs.

items.title: Tigers halves pair set for scans after limping off in loss to Rabbitohs
items.summary: Wests Tigers captain Jarome Luai is expected to be out of action for several weeks, while his halves partner is also in doubt after the duo limped off during their 20-16 loss to South Sydney.

items.title: Easy meatballs in rich tomato sauce are a versatile crowd-pleaser - ABC News
items.summary: If you are after an easy and delicious dinner to serve a few or a crowd, these meatballs in rich tomato sauce are perfect to scale up or down and perfect simply with bread and salad.

items.title: 'A bad look': Docker under fire for taunting Demons oppponent
items.summary: Fremantle's Patrick Voss may face scrutiny after he taunted Melbourne's Harrison Petty during the Dockers' 48-point win in Perth.

items.title: WA Police charge Clinton James Turner over death of woman in Banksia Grove
items.summary: A man charged with manslaughter over the death of a woman known to him, in Perth's north, remains behind bars after facing court.

items.title: Elders in Kalumburu revitalise endangered Indigenous languages
items.summary: Three Indigenous languages are spoken by a handful of people in the remote community of Kalumburu — now efforts are underway to revive them and teach the next generation.

items.title: Thousands told to evacuate as Hawaii's O'ahu island hit by severe floods
items.summary: Thousands of residents in Hawaii have been warned to evacuate towns north of Honolulu as floodwaters inundate streets and damage homes.

items.title: Is the 'big spike' in public transport usage in Perth because of high fuel prices?
items.summary: WA Premier Roger Cook says public transport usage has risen over the past three weeks, over the same period that fuel prices have skyrocketed, but it's not clear whether one increase is related to the other.

items.title: How three anonymous letters could help solve mysterious hit-and-run death
items.summary: Mark Gregory was killed in a 1991 hit-and-run collision. Now, police believe a series of anonymous letters could hold the key to unlocking the 35-year mystery.

items.title: Jury hears first week of evidence in trial of men accused of raping woman who believed they were Uber drivers
items.summary: A jury has heard a woman told two men to "get out" during an alleged sexual assault in her Darwin apartment that lasted more than eight hours. The court was told the woman had been drinking on a night out and was picked up by two men while she was waiting for an Uber to take her home.

items.title: Farmers count the cost of floods, with the industry warning of produce shortages and higher prices
items.summary: The farming industry is warning consumers that multiple weather disasters in Queensland, paired with high fuel costs, will likely limit fresh produce supply and push prices higher.

items.title: Pioneering Auslan program breaks down barriers in Canberra schoolyard
items.summary: Mount Rogers Primary School in Canberra is the only public school in the ACT to teach Australian Sign Language as its second language.

items.title: Severe Tropical Cyclone Narelle to hit NT as category three on Saturday night, affects to begin this afternoon
items.summary: After regaining strength in the gulf, Severe Tropical Cyclone Narelle is expected to make landfall in the Northern Territory on Saturday evening.

items.title: Warning system upgrades to come amid Northern Territory flood emergencies
items.summary: The Northern Territory and federal governments promise to improve flood warning systems after flood-ravaged communities say they were put at risk during rushed evacuations.

items.title: Women's Asian Cup 2026: How the goldfish mentality is helping Matildas defender Kaitlyn Torpey
items.summary: A Ted Lasso lesson, and an Australian indie rock classic, are inspiring Kaitlyn Torpey to take her career to new heights, as she prepares for tonight's Women's Asian Cup final.

items.title: Pair arrested after trying to enter naval base hosting UK's nuclear submarines
items.summary: The man and woman were arrested after trying to enter HM Naval Base Clyde on Thursday, Scottish Police say.

items.title: Quarter of Tasmanian police family violence orders issued against women, with fears victims are being misidentified
items.summary: On-the-spot family violence orders are designed to protect those impacted by domestic and family violence. But advocates say they can unfavourably backfire on victim-survivors.

items.title: Why I watch Married at First Sight with my teenage son
items.summary: Watching the largely fake, but also completely true, juggernaut reality TV series Married at First Sight with our 14-year-old was never a deliberate choice but it is potentially very powerful.

items.title: Western New South Wales farmers bear brunt of fuel redistribution
items.summary: Scott Worthington has run out of fuel, after first running out of water on his drought-stricken farm, and watching his pastures turn to dirt.

items.title: New Whitsundays Ngaro Track hiking trail fuels debate over tourism in national parks
items.summary: With only 15 walkers a day and costing $150 per person, the new Ngaro Track sparks debate about commercial tourism in Queensland's national parks.

items.title: Women's Asian Cup 2026: When do the Matildas play Japan in the final? How to watch and follow the decider
items.summary: The Matildas' quest for the Women's Asian Cup trophy reaches its climax tonight, when they face Japan at Sydney's Stadium Australia. Here's how to watch and follow the game.

items.title: Space junk is cluttering Earth's low orbit with high-velocity projectiles and it's getting worse
items.summary: Thousands of satellites are being launched into space at low Earth orbit, joining hundreds of thousands of pieces of space junk, and it's becoming a real problem.

items.title: Women's Asian Cup 2026: Young Japanese-Australians showing up loud and proud for final
items.summary: Young Japanese diaspora in Sydney will be showing up loud and proud in support of Japan against Australia's Matildas at the Asian Cup final on Saturday.

items.title: The Snowy Mountains mystery of the Southern Cloud aviation disaster
items.summary: When eight people vanished without a trace in 1931, Australia's first major airline disaster became a 27-year mystery that gripped the nation, until a hiker's chance discovery changed everything.

items.title: ABC 50/50 quiz: This week we're testing your knowledge of 2000s hip-hop, Mercury in retrograde and disturbing kids' TV puppets
items.summary: Every question has only two options: one is right and the other is wrong. You have an even chance of guessing correctly each time.

items.title: 'We've done what we've been asked': Albanese responds to Trump outburst — as it happened
items.summary: Iran's military threatens to hunt down US and Israeli officials and army commanders even while they are on holiday or visiting entertainment centres.

items.title: The government says fuel supply is safe until mid-April. What happens after that?
items.summary: The government is responding to fuel shortages due to the war in the Middle East and says Australia has enough supply until mid-April. Here's what experts say could happen after that.

items.title: Peak fashion body aims to rebuild local manufacturing battered by offshore supply chain interruptions
items.summary: Australia's peak clothing and textile organisation launches a national strategy to revitalise an industry hit by years of turmoil.

items.title: Coroner finds Worimi woman's death at Lightning Ridge hospital was preventable
items.summary: The coroner found that a mother of three could have been saved if a transfer from a remote New South Wales hospital had been ordered sooner.

items.title: Government leaves door open to gas, coal tax as companies warn it will threaten supply
items.summary: The Greens and One Nation are among those that want gas profits levied, with pressure mounting on Labor to respond to growing calls for reform.

items.title: In the Strait of Hormuz, Iran unleashes a plot against the West years in the making
items.summary: By moving so much of the war into the Strait of Hormuz and Persian Gulf, Tehran has hit its adversaries where it hurts the most and analysts warn that while the United States military is mighty, history shows in asymmetrical warfare, the lesser power can win the day.

items.title: Greig Fraser filmed the unfilmable for Dune. Now he's back for Project Hail Mary
items.summary: With most of Project Hail Mary's action taking place in far off corners of space it was up to Australian cinematographer Greig Fraser to create what we've never seen.

items.title: From pub knitting to wonky ceramics, arts and crafts are having a resurgence
items.summary: Craft trends come and go, but a resurgence in social arts and crafts is proving that creativity and human connection are still very much in vogue.

items.title: Donald Trump calls NATO 'cowards' over Strait of Hormuz as oil prices spike
items.summary: The US president claims it would be "easy" for members of the Western alliance to protect the shipping route, amid reports America plans to blockade Iran's strategically important Kharg Island.

items.title: Politics is about to become all about gas
items.summary: Scott Morrison's assertion that the chase to secure vaccines in early 2021 was "not a race" cost him dearly. A similar global contest is already underway, only this time it involves securing energy supplies.

items.title: Chuck Norris, film star and martial artist, dies aged 86 from sudden illness
items.summary: Norris's epic fight with kung fu superstar Bruce Lee in The Way of The Dragon helped him become an icon, and he went on to star in more than 20 movies.

items.title: Bulldogs 'tick off' another item on 2026 AFL season bucket list
items.summary: A brilliant Oskar Baker goal clinches a thrilling win for the Bulldogs over the Crows, leaving Luke Beveridge's side unbeaten after three matches.

items.title: Two men charged after violent assault of woman outside Darwin nightclub
items.summary: Two men have been charged over an attack outside a Darwin nightclub where a female security worker was allegedly struck with a glass and kicked in the head while unconscious.

items.title: Perth man Luke Hanif Sekkouah who murdered mother-of-four Erica Hay sentenced to life in prison
items.summary: A Perth man who murdered his partner, a mother of four, before setting her on fire has been sentenced to life in prison with a non-parole period of 24 years.

items.title: Coroner finds paramedic failures in death of Phillip Mark Walton in custody
items.summary: A coroner has found paramedics failed to meet standards of care for a man who died from cardiac arrhythmia while being restrained by police.

items.title: Katherine residents brace for second major flood in two weeks as Severe Tropical Cyclone Narelle approaches NT
items.summary: Just two weeks after its worst flood in almost 30 years, Katherine businesses are being sandbagged and a field hospital has been set up as locals brace for another major weather event.

items.title: Bellamy pinpoints Storm's downfall as Broncos achieve 10-year first
items.summary: Storm coach Craig Bellamy identifies the area in which he believes his side let themselves down, as the Broncos celebrate a rare win in Melbourne.

items.title: Federal government approves 1,695 gas wells in rural Queensland
items.summary: A major multinational gas venture has just secured federal approval to ramp up extraction in rural Queensland — locking in operations for the next 55 years.

items.title: Velvel Serebryanski, convicted of sexually abusing Manny Waks, walks free from court
items.summary: A member of Melbourne's ultra-orthodox Jewish community now living in Israel says he feels vindicated and free despite his abuser walking free from court and avoiding more jail time.

items.title: Robert Alan Gee jailed for 13 years for manslaughter of baby in caravan park
items.summary: Robert Alan Gee, who has been jailed for the manslaughter of baby Michael Willmott, had in 2003 assaulted a 3-month-old girl in a different state.

items.title: Tyrone Russell found guilty of unlawfully killing Samuel Liszczak
items.summary: A Supreme Court jury delivers a verdict in the case of two men charged over a string of public shootings on the NSW mid north coast in 2023.

items.title: Epworth releases details into hospital review following endometriosis allegations
items.summary: A South Australian health commissioner will lead the review of clinical governance at Epworth Hospital, after allegations a top gynaecologist performed unnecessary operations to remove endometriosis.

items.title: Former childcare worker Arvind Ajay Singh jailed for indecent treatment of child
items.summary: A former childcare worker from Fiji is sentenced to jail for the indecent treatment of a child at a daycare centre in Queensland, and will be deported afterwards.

items.title: Sunraysia footballer pleads guilty to trafficking more than $120k of cocaine, MDMA into Mildura
items.summary: A country footballer who "became complacent" and trafficked more than $120,000 of drugs will face the County Court after pleading guilty to all charges.

items.title: Alana King stakes claim for T20 World Cup recall with dominant performance against West Indies
items.summary: Alana King gives Australia's selectors a timely reminder of the quality she possesses, helping secure a win against the West Indies.

items.title: Trump has postponed his meeting with Xi, but China is not wasting any time on it
items.summary: Donald Trump has postponed a meeting with Xi Jinping because he is busy with Iran and the Middle East — and that's good news for China.

items.title: One Nation candidate Aoi Baxter wanted in the UK for failing to attend court on sexual touching charge
items.summary: South Australian One Nation candidate Aoi Baxter has a warrant for his arrest in the UK after he failed to show up to court to face a charge of sexually touching a woman without consent.

items.title: Ashton Hurn lives in hope of becoming SA's premier after election
items.summary: Polls predict the Liberals are heading for a wipe-out in South Australia on Saturday, but party leader Ashton Hurn says she is still working with the goal of becoming the state's leader rather than analysing what went wrong.

items.title: Unmasking Banksy is missing the point of his work. What's so wrong with a little mystery?
items.summary: Does Banksy's success mean we are more entitled to demand he pull his mask off, show his face? Does it matter that he says his anonymity is core to his art?

items.title: Poverty increased after Victoria 2022 floods while disposable income decreased, report finds
items.summary: Poverty and unemployment rose after Victoria's 2022 floods, with those in inundated areas experiencing lower disposable income than those who weren't affected, according to a new report.

items.title: Teenager Moise Kouame makes history with win over Zachary Svajda at ATP Masters Miami Open
items.summary: Teenager Moise Kouame steals the spotlight as Australians Alexei Popyrin and James Duckworth crash out of the multi-million-dollar Miami Open.

items.title: Why gas giants have suddenly gone to ground over Iran
items.summary: Are Australians about to endure a re-run of the economic pain brought on by the Ukraine war? Or something much worse?

items.title: NT cattle station Aroona sells to Argentinian family for $44 million
items.summary: An Argentinian family has expanded its portfolio of NT cattle stations with the purchase of Aroona Station.

items.title: When girls don't feel comfortable or confident in their uniforms it can cause them to drop out of sport
items.summary: Studies in Australia and overseas show a big percentage of girls drop out of sport when they become teenagers. One of the contributing factors is the clothes they have to wear.

items.title: Nathan Vikatos jailed for 28 years for murdering toddler son Alexi
items.summary: Nathan Vikatos has been jailed for more than 28 years for what a judge described as the tragic, horrific and untimely death of his three-year-old son, Alexi, in May 2023.

items.title: How the 'four right blokes' turned the ABC's coverage of the VFA into must-watch TV for footy fans
items.summary: As Aussie Rules was forced to reckon with a national competition, four men helped make the VFA must-watch TV on the ABC for Victorian footy fans.
